item.title: White House Reviews Ban on Trading Through Best Price on Stocks
item.description: The White House is reviewing whether to amend and potentially scrap a decades-old rule governing stock trading that’s meant to make sure investors get better prices for their transactions.

item.title: Delta to Prioritize Global Expansion Over Domestic Transactions
item.description: Delta Air Lines Inc. Chief Executive Officer Ed Bastian said he’ll sit out consolidation in the US aviation market and instead focus on expanding abroad through partnerships, even as high fuel prices and a transaction-minded administration stand to shake up the domestic industry.

item.title: Uber Boosts Stake in Delivery Hero, Says No Takeover for Now
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. has increased its stake in German food delivery company Delivery Hero SE, underscoring its ambitions to counter competition from DoorDash Inc. outside the US.

item.title: Vanguard Favors Treasuries as 10-Year Yields Near Range Top
item.description: Vanguard Group Inc., one of the world’s largest money managers, is sticking with its bet on US Treasuries, saying yields in the $31 trillion market are near the top of its expected range for 10-year notes.

item.title: Ramaco CEO Faults China Steel Dumping for Met-Coal Miners’ Woe
item.description: Ramaco Resources Inc.’s chief executive officer said Chinese steel dumping is hammering US miners as smelters around the world try to keep pace by demanding lower costs for metallurgical coal.

item.title: NextEra’s Mega-Utility Deal to Shore Up Its Credit Profile
item.description: NextEra Energy Inc.’s $67 billion bid for Dominion Energy Inc. won’t just create an energy giant stretching from Florida to Virginia, it’s also helping the power firm shore up its credit standing in the eyes of rating companies.

item.title: Elon Musk Loses Case Against Sam Altman Over OpenAI’s Future
item.description: A jury rejected Elon Musk’s claims that OpenAI under Sam Altman’s leadership betrayed its mission to benefit the public by morphing into a for-profit business, finding that he waited too long to sue the company.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Will Add Another Musk Stock. It’s a Problem for Tesla
item.description: For years, there was only one way for mom-and-pop investors to buy into Elon Musk’s vision: shares of Tesla Inc. That’s about to change — and it’s a serious risk for Tesla investors.

item.title: Apple Readies AI Writing Help, App Shortcuts and Wallpapers for iOS 27
item.description: Apple Inc. is preparing a wave of artificial intelligence features for its next iPhone and iPad operating systems, including a grammar checker and new shortcut options, part of an effort to narrow the gap with capabilities available on rival devices.

item.title: Brazil’s Government Now Sees Inflation Soaring Past Target for Longer on Iran War
item.description: Brazil’s Finance Ministry raised its consumer price forecasts sharply and now sees inflation running above target through at least 2027, reflecting the shock from the Middle East war.

item.title: EU Ties Part of Ukraine €90 Billion Aid to Unpopular Tax Measure
item.description: The European Union will tie some payouts from the €90 billion ($105 billion) aid package for Ukraine to an unpopular tax change already demanded by the International Monetary Fund, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Ghana Seeks 30% of Large Gold Mines’ Output For Local Refineries
item.description: Ghana wants large-scale gold mines to sell at least 30% of their annual output to the central bank to increase local refining and boost the country’s foreign-exchange reserves.

item.title: Texas Fund Revealed as Record Backer of State Street Credit ETF
item.description: The identity of an investor that dramatically boosted assets in State Street Corp.’s private credit ETF has been revealed as a $60 billion sovereign wealth fund that provides funding to Texas public schools.

item.title: Spain’s ACS Offers €2.2 Billion Stake to Fund Data Center Plans
item.description: ACS Actividades de Construcción y Servicios SA is looking to sell about 6% of its shares to fund data centers and other AI-related infrastructure.

item.title: The Shock Is Dead, Long Live the Shock!
item.description: Constant disruptions can’t really be labeled as ‘one-offs.’

item.title: Royal Caribbean Slides After Mexico Reviews Water Park Project
item.description: Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. shares fell on Monday after Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum ordered a review of the cruise line operator’s water park project in Quintana Roo state.

item.title: Europe Must Stand Up for Itself in Global Economy, Germany Says
item.description: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil said Europe must assert its own interests against global competitors with measures such as favoring companies from the region.

item.title: Dell Adds 1,000 Clients for AI Servers, Targets Corporate Users
item.description: Dell Technologies Inc. said it added 1,000 customers for a key AI product line in the past quarter as the company tries to capture business from traditional large businesses taking on new artificial intelligence workloads.

item.title: Anthropic Sends Jolt Through Market for Buying Shares in Hot Pre-IPO Startups
item.description: In the moments after Anthropic expanded a ban on popular ways to buy its shares, investor chatrooms around the world lit up. “Are we screwed?” one person wrote in a WhatsApp chat for family offices with several hundred members. Similar questions reverberated more publicly across X, Reddit and Chinese-language social media, as investors worried whether their shares in the artificial intelligence developer — one of the most coveted private companies — had suddenly become worthless.Days later, ther

item.title: West Marine Files Bankruptcy to Cut Debt, Close More Stores
item.description: Boating supply retailer West Marine Inc. filed for bankruptcy protection, citing weaker sales as consumers pull back on outdoor recreation because of inflation and several years of severe weather.

item.title: Trafigura and Tether Are Discussing USDT Usage in Fuel Stations
item.description: Commodity trading house Trafigura Group Pte Ltd. is in talks with Tether Inc. about a pilot project to use its stablecoin at fuel stations.

item.title: Companies Look to Once-Dead Caracas Stock Market as Volumes Soar
item.description: A raft of Venezuelan companies are looking to sell shares on the local stock exchange as the market wakes up after more than a decade of near paralysis under the socialist rule of former president Nicolas Maduro.

item.title: NextEra-Dominion Deal Signals New Era of AI Utility Mega-Mergers
item.description: NextEra Energy Inc. just announced the largest utility acquisition in US history, a $67 billion deal for rival Dominion Energy Inc., suggesting even the industry’s biggest companies need to grow in order to accommodate the power demand driven by artificial intelligence.

item.title: Burnham to Reject Any Change to UK Fiscal Rules If He Wins
item.description: Andy Burnham, the current favorite to replace Keir Starmer as UK prime minister, has ruled out changing the government’s self-imposed limits on borrowing if he were to gain power.

item.title: Zambia Forms Gold Venture to Formalize Mining, Build Processing
item.description: Zambia’s state-controlled mining investment firm has formed a joint venture to formalize the artisanal gold trade in a region of the southern African country.

item.title: EU Selects EQT to Manage New €5 Billion Technology Fund
item.description: EQT AB has won the mandate to manage a new European Union fund for investing in quantum computing, artificial intelligence and other deep tech companies.

item.title: Investors Flock to Commodity ETFs as Iran War Fuels Energy Inflation
item.description: Investors are pouring money into commodities funds as the US-Iran war stokes inflation, according to Invesco Ltd.

item.title: Few Foreign Ships Seen in Hormuz Amid Reports of Iran’s Insurance Plan
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial shipping, with maritime traffic reduced to a trickle and dominated by Iranian-linked vessels. There were few notable foreign-affiliated movements amid reports of Iran’s Bitcoin-backed insurance program for shipping companies willing to transit the waterway.

item.title: Tankers Entering Hormuz During Iran War Are Making Their Way Out
item.description: Almost all large non-Iranian tankers that have entered the Persian Gulf during the war appear to have successfully exited with a cargo, underscoring the emergence of a small group of shipowners willing to risk crossing the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Zambia Seen Reaping Record Corn Harvest, Bringing Gains and Risk
item.description: Zambia’s staple corn harvest will exceed 4.1 million tons this year, according to the country’s millers association, a second straight record that could help cool inflation but also poses fiscal risks.
